# Archi.Flow Frontend Skill — archiflow-frontend

Short: Assist with frontend development tasks in this repo. Enforces the Anti‑Slop design system, scaffolds feature modules, generates Server Actions & TanStack Query hooks, and runs the `skill-check` validators.

## Quick Intents & Triggers

- Scaffold new feature
  - Triggers: "Create feature <name>", "Scaffold <name> feature"
  - Behavior: scaffold `src/features/<name>/` with `types.ts`, `server/data.ts`, `server/actions.ts`, `hooks/`, `components/`, page under `app/(dashboard)/<name>/page.tsx` and run `skill-check`.

- Add server action
  - Triggers: "Add create<Thing> action", "Make server action for <name>"
  - Behavior: Add `'use server'`, Zod validation, Prisma call, and `revalidatePath('/...')` in `features/<name>/server/actions.ts`.

- Add TanStack Query hooks
  - Triggers: "Add use<name> hook", "Create query hooks for <name>"
  - Behavior: Create `hooks/use-<plural>.ts` and `hooks/use-create-<name>.ts` following queryKey conventions.

- Enforce Anti‑Slop (policy check)
  - Triggers: PR creation, `@archiflow-bot skill-check` comment, pre-commit
  - Behavior: Run validator suite and report errors/warnings. Offer auto-fix for safe class replacements.

- Auto-fix class issues
  - Triggers: explicit user action `--auto-fix` or reply "auto-fix"
  - Behavior: Apply safe replacements (e.g., `rounded-full` → `rounded-[2px]`), create a branch/commit, re-run `skill-check` and present diff.

## Enforcement Rules (high level)

Block or flag the following (examples):
- Forbidden fonts: `Inter`, `Roboto`, `San Francisco`, `Open Sans`, `Segoe UI`
- Forbidden classes: `rounded-full`, `bg-white` on canvas-level layouts, `shadow-lg|shadow-xl` (soft shadows), `blur-3xl`, and ``bg-gradient-to-*` with pink/purple blobs
- Required patterns: `font-display`, `font-sans`, `font-mono`, `rounded-[2px]`, `border border-zinc-900`, `shadow-[4px_4px_0px_0px_rgba(0,0,0,1)]` for interactive elements
- Server actions: must include `'use server'`, Zod validation, and `revalidatePath('/...')` when modifying data
- Import rules: Feature modules may import `components/ui` and `components/custom`, but not other feature directories

## Dry-run & Auto-fix UX

- Dry-run: provide a concise list of errors and warnings and a suggested diff for each fix.
- Auto-fix: apply only safe transformations (class name replacements). For complex fixes (layout changes, semantic changes) refuse auto-apply and require PR + human review.

## Files referenced by skill

- Scaffolding: `src/features/{name}/types.ts`, `server/data.ts`, `server/actions.ts`, `hooks/`, `components/`, `app/(dashboard)/{name}/page.tsx`
- Validators: `tools/skill-validators/*.js`
- CI: `.github/workflows/skill-check.yml`

## Examples (brief)

- "Create feature tasks" → creates files above and runs `skill-check`, returns pass/fail and suggested next steps.
- "Enforce design on ProjectCard" → runs validator, returns proposed diffs and (if chosen) applies safe fixes.

## Notes

- Skill is conservative by default — prefer a dry-run and PR workflow for anything that changes architecture or DB schema.
- Include tests and snapshot checks for generated scaffolding.
